title: grossulariaceae
intro: The grossulariaceae rank refers to a family of flowering plants. This family, commonly known as the currant family, consists of approximately 30-40 species of shrubs and small trees, primarily native to the Northern Hemisphere. The grossulariaceae family includes species such as black currants, golden currants, and gooseberries, which are known for their edible fruits and ornamental value. The plants within this family are typically deciduous, producing white or pink flowers followed by berries that vary in color depending on the species.
